qas.q: If there is an "RQ" before or after the item description on the shipping paper, it means the:
qas.a: Carrier must report a spill of this material.

qas.q: When hauling packages of explosives, you must:
qas.a: Never use hooks or other metal tools.

qas.q: Your vehicle contains explosives, oxidizers or flammables. No one may smoke within.....feet of the vehicle.
You should stop before crossing a railroad grade if your vehicle is carrying.....of chlorine.
When shippers package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to the regulations. The only exception is when:
qas.a: The shipment is a hazardous waste.
Must put the word "waste" before the proper shipping name of material on the shipping paper.

qas.q: The basic description of a description of a hazardous material includes the hazard class, the identification number and the shipping name. Which one must appear first on the shipping paper?
When are you allowed to move an improperly placarded vehicle carrying hazardous materials?
qas.a: During an emergency, in order to protect life and property.

qas.q: You are transporting hazardous materials. When you are NOT behind the wheel, the shipping papers must be on the driver's seat or:
You are carrying Class 7 (radioactive) material. Must you stop before railroad crossings?
If hazardous material is spilling from your vehicle, move your vehicle:
To find out if a shipment includes a hazardous material, you should:

qas.a: An approved place for parking unattended trucks loaded with explosives.

qas.q: A properly prepared Uniform Hazardous Waste Manifest:
qas.a: Must be signed and carried by anyone transporting a hazardous waste.

qas.q: The power unit of a placarded vehicle must have a fire extinguisher with a UL Rating of.....BC or more.
You need to warn of a stopped vehicle which has a tank used for flammable liquids or games. You may use:
If you are in an accident involving hazardous materials, you should:
qas.a: Keep all people far away and upwind of the accident.
